staticStrings.outputTableIntro: These values will appear in the output table to help you identify the trees.
staticStrings.howDoYouWantToDisplay: How do you want to display...
staticStrings.theProportionOfSuccesses: ... what proportion of dots are blue?
staticStrings.sAutomaticallyShowSplitPointsInBranchLab: automatically show split points in branch labels

arbor.sYourBestGuess: your best guess for these cases:
arbor.sLeafNoDiagnosis: You have not assigned a diagnosis yet. Click to assign!

attributeDescriptions.sadPredict: what does this tree try to predict?
attributeDescriptions.sadNPPos: number of positives without a prediction
attributeDescriptions.sadNPNeg: number of negatives without a prediction
attributeDescriptions.sadSensitivity: sensitivity (calculated): the proportion of positive cases that are diagnosed positive
attributeDescriptions.sadMisclassificationRate: misclassification rate: the proportion of predictions we did not get correct
attributeDescriptions.sadSSModel: Sum of squares of residuals in the model
attributeDescriptions.sadSSTotal: Total sum of squares of residuals
attributeDescriptions.sadExplained: Percent of variance explained by the model
attributeDescriptions.sadSumSSD: total (normalized) sum of the squares of the deviations

stripeToolTips.plusMinus: change the diagnosis in this 'leaf' node from + to – or vice versa
stripeToolTips.trash: remove the children of this node (i.e., get rid of •1• here)
stripeToolTips.dependent: change positive diagnoses to negative and vice versa

arbor.sPositiveNegativeNodeDescription: In this scenario, we call (•1•) 'positive' and (•2•) 'negative'.
arbor.sRegressionRootNodeDescription: In this scenario, we are estimating the mean of •1•.
arbor.sRegressionNodeMeanDescription: For these cases, the mean of •1• is •2•, and the SSR is •3•
